edges.description: Reduced PNPLA6/NTE esterase activity produces the tissue-vulnerability phenotype continuum across PNPLA6 disorders.
edges.source: Loss of neuropathy target esterase (NTE) activity

edges.description: Cerebellar neuronal vulnerability manifests as cerebellar ataxia.
Cerebellar neurodegeneration is visible as cerebellar atrophy on MRI.
Hypothalamic-pituitary vulnerability manifests as hypogonadotropic hypogonadism.
Endocrine involvement can extend from isolated hypogonadotropic hypogonadism to anterior hypopituitarism.
Retinal vulnerability manifests as chorioretinal dystrophy.
Progressive retinal disease causes visual impairment across the PNPLA6 spectrum.
PNPLA6 neurodegenerative involvement can extend to peripheral axons.
Upper-motor-neuron involvement in PNPLA6 disorders can manifest as spasticity.
CNS involvement in the PNPLA6 spectrum includes cognitive impairment in a subset of patients.
Cerebellar and ocular involvement can produce nystagmus.

edges.target: Loss of neuropathy target esterase (NTE) activity

nodes.description: In a subset of patients, hypogonadotropic hypogonadism occurs as part of broader anterior hypopituitarism with additional growth hormone, thyroid hormone, or gonadotropin deficiencies.

evidence.reference_title: PNPLA6 Disorders.
evidence.snippet: The hypogonadotropic hypogonadism occurs either in isolation or as part of anterior hypopituitarism (growth hormone, thyroid hormone, or gonadotropin deficiencies).

nodes.description: Cerebellar ataxia/cerebellar degeneration is a core feature, with onset ranging from childhood to adulthood and typically slow progression; brain MRI commonly shows cerebellar (often superior vermian) atrophy.

evidence.reference_title: Compound heterozygous PNPLA6 mutations cause Boucher-Neuhäuser syndrome with late-onset ataxia.
evidence.snippet: Boucher-Neuhäuser is a rare autosomal recessive syndrome characterized by the co-occurrence of cerebellar ataxia, hypogonadotropic hypogonadism, and chorioretinal dystrophy.

evidence.reference_title: Multifaceted and Age-Dependent Phenotypes Associated With Biallelic PNPLA6 Gene Variants: Eight Novel Cases and Review of the Literature.
evidence.snippet: Cerebellar ataxia was observed in seven patients and spastic paraplegia in one patient.

nodes.description: Brain MRI shows cerebellar atrophy, most pronounced in the superior and dorsal vermis.

evidence.reference_title: Multifaceted and Age-Dependent Phenotypes Associated With Biallelic PNPLA6 Gene Variants: Eight Novel Cases and Review of the Literature.
evidence.snippet: Brain MRI showed cerebellar atrophy in 6/8 patients, more pronounced in superior and dorsal vermis lobules (I to VII).

evidence.reference_title: Detailed retinal phenotype of Boucher-Neuhäuser syndrome associated with mutations in PNPLA6 mimicking choroideremia.
evidence.snippet: Hypogonadotropic hypogonadism and cerebellar vermis hypoplasia by MRI confirmed a diagnosis of Boucher-Neuhäuser syndrome.

nodes.description: Chorioretinal dystrophy with progressive retinal pigment epithelium and choriocapillaris atrophy; onset is widely variable and the presentation can closely mimic choroideremia, sometimes progressing to severe visual loss or blindness.

evidence.reference_title: Compound heterozygous PNPLA6 mutations cause Boucher-Neuhäuser syndrome with late-onset ataxia.
evidence.snippet: Boucher-Neuhäuser is a rare autosomal recessive syndrome characterized by the co-occurrence of cerebellar ataxia, hypogonadotropic hypogonadism, and chorioretinal dystrophy.

evidence.reference_title: PNPLA6 disorders: what's in a name?
evidence.snippet: Retinal disease presents with a unique chorioretinal dystrophy that is phenotypically similar to choroideremia and Leber congenital amaurosis.

nodes.description: Impaired cognitive functioning (learning disabilities in children; deficits in attention, visuospatial abilities, and recall in adults) occurs in a subset of patients.

evidence.reference_title: PNPLA6 Disorders.
evidence.snippet: impaired cognitive functioning (learning disabilities in children; deficits in attention, visuospatial abilities, and recall in adults)

nodes.description: Hypogonadotropic hypogonadism presents as delayed puberty and lack of secondary sexual characteristics, with low gonadotropins (LH/FSH). It may occur in isolation or as part of broader anterior hypopituitarism.

evidence.reference_title: Compound heterozygous PNPLA6 mutations cause Boucher-Neuhäuser syndrome with late-onset ataxia.
evidence.snippet: Boucher-Neuhäuser is a rare autosomal recessive syndrome characterized by the co-occurrence of cerebellar ataxia, hypogonadotropic hypogonadism, and chorioretinal dystrophy.

evidence.reference_title: PNPLA6 Disorders.
evidence.snippet: hypogonadotropic hypogonadism (delayed puberty and lack of secondary sex characteristics)

nodes.description: PNPLA6 encodes neuropathy target esterase (NTE), a patatin-like serine hydrolase on the cytoplasmic face of the endoplasmic reticulum with phospholipase/lysophospholipase activity. NTE deacylates glycerophospholipids and is central to phosphatidylcholine homeostasis. Biallelic pathogenic PNPLA6 variants, which cluster in the C-terminal phospholipase/esterase (patatin-like) domain, reduce NTE esterase activity, the principal loss-of-function mechanism underlying BNS.
nodes.id: Loss of neuropathy target esterase (NTE) activity

evidence.reference_title: PNPLA6 disorders: what's in a name?
evidence.snippet: PNPLA6 encodes NTE, an enzyme involved in maintaining phospholipid homeostasis and trafficking in the nervous system.

evidence.reference_title: Neuropathy target esterase activity defines phenotypes among PNPLA6 disorders.
evidence.snippet: Measuring esterase activity of 46 disease-associated and 20 common variants observed across PNPLA6-associated clinical diagnoses unambiguously reclassified 36 variants as pathogenic and 10 variants as likely pathogenic

nodes.description: Reduced NTE activity disrupts phospholipid remodeling at ER membranes, rendering specific neuronal and supporting cell populations vulnerable to age-dependent degeneration: cerebellar Purkinje-cell circuits (ataxia), retinal photoreceptors and retinal pigment epithelium (chorioretinal dystrophy), and hypothalamic-pituitary neurons (hypogonadotropic hypogonadism / anterior hypopituitarism). Overall NTE activity is inversely related to the presence of retinopathy and endocrinopathy, defining a genotype:activity:phenotype continuum across PNPLA6 disorders. An allelic mouse series recapitulates an NTE activity threshold for retinal degeneration, with complete loss being embryonic lethal.

evidence.reference_title: Neuropathy target esterase activity defines phenotypes among PNPLA6 disorders.
evidence.snippet: Estimating the overall NTE activity of affected individuals revealed a striking inverse relationship between NTE activity and the presence of retinopathy and endocrinopathy.

evidence.reference_title: Neuropathy target esterase activity defines phenotypes among PNPLA6 disorders.
evidence.snippet: This phenomenon was recaptured in vivo in an allelic mouse series, where a similar NTE threshold for retinopathy exists.

evidence.reference_title: PNPLA6/NTE, an Evolutionary Conserved Phospholipase Linked to a Group of Complex Human Diseases.
evidence.snippet: A conditional knockout of PNPLA6/NTE in the mouse brain results in age-related neurodegeneration, whereas a complete knockout causes lethality during embryogenesis due to defects in the development of the placenta.

nodes.description: Oculomotor abnormalities including gaze-evoked nystagmus and saccadic pursuit have been reported.

evidence.reference_title: Compound heterozygous PNPLA6 mutations cause Boucher-Neuhäuser syndrome with late-onset ataxia.
evidence.snippet: She had hypometric vertical and horizontal saccades, saccadic smooth pursuit, gaze-evoked nystagmus and poor visual suppression of vestibular ocular reflexes.

evidence.reference_title: Identification of Novel Compound Heterozygous Variants of the PNPLA6 Gene in Boucher-Neuhäuser Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Boucher-Neuhäuser syndrome (BNS, MIM 215470) is a rare autosomal recessive syndrome caused by mutations in the PNPLA6 gene.

evidence.reference_title: Compound heterozygous PNPLA6 mutations cause Boucher-Neuhäuser syndrome with late-onset ataxia.
evidence.snippet: both mutations (one novel and one known) fell in the phospholipase esterase domain, where most pathogenic mutations seem to cluster

evidence.reference_title: Multifaceted and Age-Dependent Phenotypes Associated With Biallelic PNPLA6 Gene Variants: Eight Novel Cases and Review of the Literature.
evidence.snippet: A wide spectrum of neurodegenerative diseases has been associated with pathogenic variants in the PNPLA6 (patatin-like phospholipase domain-containing protein 6) gene, including spastic paraplegia type 39, Gordon-Holmes, Boucher-Neuhauser, Oliver-Mc Farlane, and Laurence-Moon syndromes.

nodes.description: A peripheral neuropathy of axonal type (reduced distal reflexes, diminished vibratory sensation, and/or distal muscle wasting) is a common but less frequent feature; it may be subclinical and detected only on EMG/NCS.

evidence.reference_title: Multifaceted and Age-Dependent Phenotypes Associated With Biallelic PNPLA6 Gene Variants: Eight Novel Cases and Review of the Literature.
evidence.snippet: Additional clinical features included hypogonadotropic hypogonadism (5/8), growth hormone deficiency (2/8), peripheral axonal neuropathy (4/8), cognitive impairment (3/8), chorioretinal dystrophy (2/8)

nodes.description: Upper motor neuron involvement manifesting as spasticity and/or brisk reflexes occurs along the PNPLA6 phenotypic continuum.

evidence.reference_title: PNPLA6 Disorders.
evidence.snippet: upper motor neuron involvement manifesting as spasticity and/or brisk reflexes

nodes.description: Progressive chorioretinal degeneration leads to variable visual impairment, which may progress to blindness.

evidence.reference_title: Neuropathy target esterase activity defines phenotypes among PNPLA6 disorders.
evidence.snippet: Biallelic pathogenic variants in the PNPLA6 gene cause a broad spectrum of disorders leading to gait disturbance, visual impairment, anterior hypopituitarism and hair anomalies.
